source.author: Mark Newton CMT
source.affiliation: Fundstrat — Global Head of Technical Strategy

text:
IBM plunge might have caught many off-guard, but there were certainly signs of $IBM starting to trade a lot more erratically as of early last year beginning with a series of deep retracements

Then, the IBM pullback from Nov '25 into May '26 overbalanced in time & undercut the last major Swing low. Despite a subsequent robust shakeout back to new highs, the broader structure had already begun to weaken quite a bit in morphing from an intermediate-term bull into more of a multi-yr sideways pattern & monthly MACD rolled over to negative early this year.

Volume also began to show much greater signs of distribution & spiking on many of the "down" days in recent months. It goes without saying that $212 now becomes critically important, though right above a 50% retracement of the low-to-high range since '20. No position & important to let this stabilize before trying to be the hero.

iffy_tells.summary: Series of deep retracements — price starts trading erratically vs prior clean trend.
Corrective leg overbalances prior corrective legs in duration (Nov '25 → May '26).
Pullback undercuts the prior major swing low — structural damage, not healthy digression.
Robust shakeout/rally back to new highs AFTER the structure already weakened — classic trap for late bulls.
Character morph: intermediate-term bull → multi-year sideways / distribution regime.
Monthly MACD rolls negative — higher-timeframe momentum confirms deterioration.
Volume spikes on down days; distribution character in recent months.
Key support ($212) sits just above the 50% retracement of the 2020 low-to-high range — lost zone = further damage.
No position; let price stabilize before catching falling knives.

operator_note: Canonical example of spotting an iffy situation: new highs after structural damage are not confirmation; they can be the last shakeout before a plunge. Prefer HTF deterioration (monthly MACD, regime morph, distribution volume) over the most recent up-leg.
tt_system_hooks: Investor weekly Phase DISTRIBUTION already warns (worker/investor.js).
phase_left_distribution / setup-sequence stage used in foundation mining.
Gap: no first-class 'shakeout-to-new-highs after swing undercut + HTF MACD rollover' composite warn for CIO/rail thesis.
Useful as a CIO reference vignette when defending REDUCE/WATCH vs chasing a reclaim at ATH.
